reviewed_actions.ai_reasoning: Highest quant score on the watchlist at 68. Volume score of 96 and volatility score of 96 indicate explosive participation and movement potential. Price momentum at 64 is solid. Healthcare sector showing relative strength. Revenue growth of 23.1% with strong gross margins. This is the kind of high-volume, high-volatility momentum name this strategy targets. In a weak bear regime, high-volume names with strong price momentum are the ones most likely to decouple from the index.

Price momentum score of 90 is the second highest on the entire watchlist — this is a name clearly being bought hard and trending near highs. Energy has been a relative strength leader. In bear regimes, energy and defensives tend to hold up or even rally. XOM's momentum tape is screaming strength despite low overall quant score — the momentum factor is what matters for this strategy DNA. Lower volatility score (66) actually means cleaner trending behavior.

Down -2% after 1 day, momentum is stalling immediately. In a max-aggression momentum strategy, we don't hold losers. The capital is better deployed into EXAS and XOM which have significantly stronger momentum profiles. CORT shows no volume surge or breakout characteristics. Freeing up capital to go heavier into higher-conviction momentum names.

Down -4.5% after just 1 day — this is anti-momentum. The strategy DNA says sell fast if momentum stalls, and a -4.5% immediate drawdown is not stalling, it's failing. DOCN is a tech name in a bear regime with no volume confirmation of a bounce. Reallocating this capital to names with actual price momentum (XOM at 90 momentum score) is the correct move.
